126395.explanation: We choose careful decision-making and administrative responsibility. Important choices belong in the municipal council, with good participation and input in advance from residents and entrepreneurs, not through a simplistic yes/no referendum.

126398.explanation: Complete freedom does not fit. Hospitality is important but also affects livability, order, and safety. The municipality sets clear frameworks and already provides room through exemptions and extended hours on collective holidays such as Carnival and New Year's Eve.

126403.explanation: We prefer a realistic and proportional approach. Open fireplaces and wood stoves belong to living comfort and freedom of choice. A generic ban is unenforceable and does not account for differences in use, circumstances, and alternatives.

126406.explanation: Events and parties strengthen livability, social interaction, and the local economy. Stricter noise standards unnecessarily complicate organizing. We consider neighbors and limit nuisance within applicable national standards.

126407.explanation: Accessibility is important, but subsidizing business premises does not fit responsible use of public funds. Entrepreneurs are themselves responsible for their buildings and operations.

126409.explanation: Good and safe school buildings are essential for quality education. We invest in improvements and new construction, with flexible and scalable buildings. In the coming years, we will realize three new child centers: Landgoed Driessen, De Veste, and De Hoef.

126410.explanation: Sports clubs are the glue of our society. If clubs struggle, the municipality helps selectively, for example through lower hall rent, collective insurance, and good municipally owned facilities.

126413.explanation: Camera surveillance contributes to safety and prevention. Together with neighborhood police and enforcement officers, it forms an important tool against nuisance and crime. We deploy camera surveillance targeted and carefully, where it demonstrably contributes to safety.

126414.explanation: Victims must be received quickly and safely, but a dedicated facility is not cost-effective for Waalwijk. A regional approach offers more expertise and capacity. We focus on good agreements, fast referral, and support close to home.

126415.explanation: Enforcement officers must be able to do their work safely. We support responsible expansion of powers and resources such as a baton, appropriate to their tasks and with clear frameworks for deployment and training.

126420.explanation: Those who can contribute make a social contribution. Those who (temporarily) cannot receive support. We encourage participation and development suited to what someone can do.

126421.explanation: The income threshold has recently been raised to 130% of the social minimum. This already helps prevent poverty and avoids more intensive assistance. We first carefully examine the effects before considering further expansion.

126423.explanation: Electric driving is growing and requires charging infrastructure, but public space is scarce. Additional charging spots must not increase parking pressure. The number of charging stations has more than doubled in recent years. Further expansion requires customized solutions and assessment per location.

126424.explanation: In residential areas, 30 km/h is the standard for safety and livability. On main roads, 50 km/h remains necessary for good flow. This way, we combine safe neighborhoods with an accessible municipality.

126427.explanation: Care must be respectful and appropriate, with attention to everyone’s background. However, full freedom of choice is not always feasible within social support and youth care, where staff availability, capacity, and affordability are limited.

126429.explanation: Period poverty is a real problem, but general free distribution is often symbolic. We opt for a targeted approach through poverty schemes, schools, and welfare organizations, so help reaches those who need it.

126431.explanation: Dog nuisance must be addressed with enforcement. We focus on facilities for dogs such as dog parks and also on enforcement. Both cost money and justify a dog tax. We do not make an automatic choice here but carefully weigh effectiveness and fairness.

126433.explanation: Expansion is not a goal in itself. We choose quality over quantity, with space for SMEs and knowledge industries. First, better use of existing sites; completion of Haven 8 East and expansion of Waspik/Scharloo is possible, but without massive box-like buildings.

126435.explanation: Social housing is scarce and must be fairly distributed. We fulfill our legal task for status holders but do not grant priority. Starters, families, and seniors often already wait too long; priority damages the sense of justice and cohesion.

126437.explanation: Solar panels belong on roofs, not on farmland. We encourage companies and residents to use roofs and to use space smarter, for example above parking lots. This way, we protect the landscape and promote sustainable energy.

126438.explanation: The library is growing from a book lender to a social meeting place. As a place where knowledge, culture, and society come together, it deserves renewal and a central, recognizable role in the heart of Waalwijk.

126440.explanation: Heritage is valuable for the identity and origin of our municipality. However, we believe the content of the education program is not a municipal task. It is up to the schools themselves to decide which choices they make.

126441.explanation: Banning solves nothing. We support legalization instead of tolerance, so the chain becomes transparent and controllable. The number of coffee shops must fit local needs and the scale of the municipality.

126442.explanation: We apply a minimum of 30% social housing as already agreed in the regional Housing Deal. That matches the housing needs and growth of Waalwijk.

126444.explanation: Room rental is limited in Waalwijk and does not require a general ban. We focus on owner-occupation to prevent buy-ups for profit and thus protect housing opportunities for starters and low-income people.

126445.explanation: Parking policy must, in principle, cover costs and contribute to an accessible and livable center. The current rate is appropriate and makes it possible to make responsible choices and invest in parking facilities such as parking garages.

126446.explanation: Building requires sufficient parking space, tailored to residents' needs. In neighborhoods, we ensure a balance with green and play areas. In the center, we opt for parking garages so that accessibility and livability go hand in hand.

126448.explanation: Waalwijk may grow, but growth is not a goal in itself. The level of amenities and quality of life must not come under pressure. Livability and capacity come before achieving a population number.

126449.explanation: The Shoe Quarter is a cultural flagship that contributes to identity, education, and vibrancy and now requires structural support. However, we expect entrepreneurship, more own income, and no further subsidy increase.

126450.explanation: We do not choose a division between villages and the center. Each area has its own challenges. Investments must be determined by necessity and effect. The villages deserve attention, but the center also serves an important function for the entire municipality.

126395.explanation: The local VVD believes in a democracy where residents elect the municipal council to make decisions. Important topics are often too complex for a simple 'yes' or 'no'. With referenda, you cannot improve plans, only reject or support them - that does not fit that role.

126398.explanation: As far as the local VVD is concerned, entrepreneurs themselves should decide when they are open. We support extended opening hours, so there is more space for evening socializing. We do this in good coordination with residents and emergency services so that vibrancy and livability go hand in hand.

126403.explanation: As a resident, you should be able to choose whether to use an open fireplace or wood stove. The local VVD believes this should remain allowed. Especially in an uncertain world, it is important that residents have alternative ways to heat their homes.

126406.explanation: The rules for noise at festivals and parties are already strict, and rightly so. As far as we are concerned, these rules do not need to become even stricter. However, the municipality must enforce when organizations break the rules, so events can continue without extra restrictions.

126407.explanation: The local VVD wants the municipality to mainly help entrepreneurs by thinking along, offering practical advice, and simplifying rules. This allows entrepreneurs to keep room to make their own choices. Therefore, we do not consider standard financial support from the municipality necessary.

126409.explanation: Good school buildings are a core task of the municipality. In parts of Waalwijk, Sprang-Capelle, and Waspik, space is already lacking or buildings are outdated. The local VVD believes active investment should be made in the coming years for safe, future-proof schools.

126410.explanation: Sports clubs are important for our community and operate on volunteers. The local VVD fully supports our clubs. They are basically self-reliant, but where needed and there is social added value, we will always consider targeted and temporary support.

126413.explanation: As far as the local VVD is concerned, CCTV surveillance should be expanded where it helps tackle nuisance and crime. Therefore, we allocate extra funding for this. More cameras increase the feeling of safety on the street and assist the police in tracking down perpetrators.

126414.explanation: The safety of women is non-negotiable for the local VVD. Everyone must feel safe, also at home. Therefore, we support shelter for those fleeing domestic violence and want to deal with offenders more harshly. Violence should never remain behind closed doors; it must be stopped.

126415.explanation: With the right training and equipment, including a baton, our enforcement officers can perform their work better and defend themselves. The local VVD stands for the safety of our emergency workers.

126420.explanation: Participation in Waalwijk must be the only option. Those who receive benefits and can contribute, in the local VVD’s view, must give something in return. Those who cannot receive help. Those who can but refuse must face consequences. This keeps the system fair for everyone who works hard.

126421.explanation: Work must always pay more than not working. The local VVD does not want to raise the income threshold further. The best way to combat poverty is work: more jobs and opportunities. People who really need help are supported targeted through existing provisions.

126423.explanation: Electric driving is increasing. The local VVD therefore wants to enable more charging points, but in balance with sufficient regular parking spaces. Parking must remain accessible and workable for everyone.

126424.explanation: Our streets are not racetracks. The local VVD more often chooses 30 km/h where it increases safety and livability. On wide, well-designed main roads, 50 km/h can remain where safe. This way, we make a careful, balanced choice.

126427.explanation: Personal choice in care is important, but according to the local VVD, it should not revolve around religion or culture. Care must be about quality and professionalism for everyone. Separate care organizations increase divisions and hinder integration. Everyone must maintain equal access.

126429.explanation: Free tampons and sanitary pads are already available to those who really need them through existing initiatives in our municipality. Therefore, the local VVD does not see the need for the municipality to create a separate scheme for this. We prefer to connect with what already works well.

126431.explanation: The local VVD wants to abolish the dog tax. This tax is outdated and mainly a revenue source. After all, there is no cat tax. Instead of new or higher taxes, the municipality should work more efficiently itself, so costs for residents remain as low as possible.

126433.explanation: The local VVD wants the strongest business climate in the region. Entrepreneurs must be able to grow and create jobs. Therefore, we offer space for expansion. Economic growth keeps our facilities (such as library, schools, and sports) affordable. The money must always be earned first.

126435.explanation: As far as the local VVD is concerned, status holders do not get priority for social housing. Our own residents should be served first. Therefore, we want to adjust the policy and create basic transition locations for status holders, without harming the housing chances of youth and starters.

126437.explanation: According to the local VVD, solar panels especially belong on roofs, not on fertile farmland. There is still enough space on company and residential roofs. Solar fields on farmland damage landscape and food production. Therefore, we encourage solar panels on roofs.

126438.explanation: The current library functions well. As far as the local VVD is concerned, an expensive renovation or relocation is not necessary right now. Only if another location is clearly better and involves hardly any extra cost will we consider it. We handle taxpayers' money responsibly.

126440.explanation: The local VVD values the dialect and local culture but does not find it necessary for the municipality to promote this at all schools. We support initiatives from schools or associations that want to pay attention to this on their own initiative.

126441.explanation: As far as the local VVD is concerned, there will be no new coffee shops. More coffee shops increase the chance of nuisance, which we want to firmly oppose. We also choose order and a tough approach to drug crime. For example, the local VVD calls for hefty fines for drug dealers.

126442.explanation: A fixed percentage of 35% social housing sounds appealing but often causes construction projects to stall or not proceed at all. Then nothing gets built. The local VVD wants more and faster construction, with room for different housing types. More space, not more rules.

126444.explanation: The local VVD does not want single-room rentals in regular residential neighborhoods. This harms livability. Labor migrants belong in good, large-scale, and quality housing at suitable locations, such as on the north side of the A59, not squeezed into residential neighborhoods.

126445.explanation: According to the local VVD, paid parking in the center should become cheaper. We want to make visiting the center more attractive. Focusing on lower parking costs should always be the goal because this attracts more visitors, helps entrepreneurs, and ensures a lively city center.

126446.explanation: More parking spaces in the neighborhoods are a top priority for the local VVD. Hardworking residents must be able to park their car close to home. In areas with high parking pressure, we want to invest targetedly so that parking becomes practical and accessible again for residents.

126448.explanation: The local VVD believes Waalwijk should grow towards 55,000 inhabitants. Growth is necessary to keep our facilities (such as schools, sports, healthcare) affordable. This means we must build enough homes so that this growth happens responsibly and sustainably.

126449.explanation: As far as the local VVD is concerned, the subsidy for the Shoe Quarter can be reduced. Currently, the municipality pays €43 subsidy per visitor for an average ticket of about €12.50. That is too high. The museum should become financially stronger and more independent, with less reliance on taxpayers' money.

126450.explanation: Waalwijk is more than just the center. The local VVD wants more money for neighborhoods and districts, as we already invest a lot in the center. This way, we make all districts more beautiful, safer, and more livable, with targeted investments in greenery, parking, and safety in Waalwijk, Sprang-Capelle, and Waspik.

126395.explanation: We find the voice of the residents very important. Therefore, we like to focus on citizen participation in council proposals within the framework of participation. We support initiatives like the citizen's budget. However, a referendum simplifies a decision to a yes/no choice, which we find unsuitable.

126398.explanation: Closing times may be determined by the municipality. These guarantee quiet times within the municipality, which benefits livability.

126403.explanation: Weather conditions and air pollution must be taken into account. A total ban on fireplaces and wood stoves is not necessary, but burning should be discouraged in unsuitable weather.

126406.explanation: As long as festivals and parties comply with applicable standards, there is no reason to intervene. Provided they are not too frequent, such events belong to a lively city. Of course, consideration must be given to residents and flora and fauna in the vicinity.

126407.explanation: Entrepreneurs are responsible for this themselves.

126409.explanation: Maintenance of school buildings is high on the priority list. When improvements are needed at a school to organize a healthy learning environment, consultations can take place with school boards.

126410.explanation: Sports clubs are enormously important. They are places where people exercise, meet each other, and build community. Clubs can always engage in dialogue with the municipality to contribute ideas. Financial support is not the main goal here.

126413.explanation: The safety of our residents is of paramount importance. At this time, there is no reason to believe that adding cameras increases safety. On the contrary, the lack of privacy can actually give an unsafe feeling.

126414.explanation: Waalwijk collaborates in this within the Hart van Brabant region. It is important that there are enough places available in women's shelters. If an additional location is needed, this can be discussed regionally, and GroenLinksaf believes we must take our responsibility.

126415.explanation: The monopoly on violence lies with the police. If tasks involve a suspected risk of violence, they must be carried out by the police. Arming the public order officers (BOAs) in Waalwijk does not make these tasks easier or more effectively executable in any way.

126420.explanation: When people receive welfare benefits, it is good to look together at whether they can participate and how that is appropriate. That is good for themselves and their surroundings. However, this is not feasible for everyone. There must be no coercion.

126421.explanation: People with a low income depend on schemes to make ends meet. It is becoming increasingly difficult for lower middle incomes as well. Because financial stress has major consequences for wellbeing, it is important that the municipality supports residents in this.

126423.explanation: At present, there is no shortage of charging points for electric cars in our municipality. This must be well monitored, and where necessary, scaled up. For new charging points, parking spaces under solar panels powered by green energy can be considered.

126424.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h in built-up areas benefits the livability of neighborhoods and districts and is better for safety. Some through roads can be exempted from this.

126427.explanation: The priority must be that you receive care when you need it. It is important that care providers can work in a culturally sensitive manner. However, it is not feasible to always have care providers available from all religions, cultures, or convictions.

126429.explanation: There are a number of locations in our municipality where free tampons and sanitary products are available. The municipality supports these points, and it is important that the municipality continues to do so.

126431.explanation: The municipality provides facilities for dogs, which must be paid for. The dog tax is an appropriate instrument for this.

126433.explanation: GroenLinksaf does not want further expansion of business parks. Restructuring or infill can be considered. But we have business activities for a city of more than 100,000 inhabitants, and a large part of this can only exist based on cheap labor migration.

126435.explanation: We have a housing obligation for new status holders. We can only fulfill this obligation if we give new status holders priority on social rental housing. If we do not do this, refugee locations will continue to become overcrowded, which negatively affects livability in those municipalities.

126437.explanation: GroenLinksaf stands for the generation of sustainable energy. We also support solar panels on roofs. However, banning solar panels on agricultural land goes too far.

126438.explanation: The library has gained new functions in recent years, including support functions for residents. For example, there is a digital information point and assistance with managing digital matters. Therefore, it is important to support the library in this.

126440.explanation: It is important that the culture of Waalwijk, Sprang-Capelle, and Waspik is also known by the younger generation. Primary schools are free to focus on this.

126441.explanation: Currently, there is one coffee shop in the municipality. It is important to ensure that as few people as possible are pushed to the illegal circuit and that there is as much cooperation as possible with the environment, police, and municipality. This way, we ensure that safety and livability are not threatened.

126442.explanation: In the municipality of Waalwijk, we strive for at least 30% social rental housing, while Casade figures show that 48% of our residents fall into that target group.

126444.explanation: In the municipality of Waalwijk, room-by-room rentals are largely linked to labor migration. In practice, room rentals cause undesirable situations. Residents have too little living space, and neighbors suffer from many extra traffic movements.

126445.explanation: Parking fees must be cost-neutral for the municipality. The municipality must realize and maintain parking spaces, and these costs must be paid. Moreover, research repeatedly shows that paid parking hardly affects visits to the center of Waalwijk.

126446.explanation: We have agreed on a parking standard within the municipality. We must comply with this. Additionally, residents can also park outside their own neighborhood to increase the livability of neighborhoods. We can only use our space once; housing and green areas take priority.

126448.explanation: Growth in the number of inhabitants in our municipality will eventually help maintain facilities. But growth should not be a goal in itself.

126449.explanation: Culture and our rich history come together in the Shoe Quarter. Therefore, the Shoe Quarter is important to the community, and the municipality has invested a lot of money in it. To stop this now would be illogical.

126450.explanation: Money in our municipality must be distributed fairly.

126395.explanation: Samen Waalwijk believes that residents should have more influence on major decisions. A referendum can help with this, but only as part of broader participation: first develop together, and for truly major choices, allow residents to decide directly.

126398.explanation: Samen Waalwijk considers the current opening hours sufficiently generous, although exceptions should remain possible for events. In addition, the livability around hospitality businesses must be well protected, for example through attention to noise, safety, and neighborhood peace.

126403.explanation: Samen Waalwijk does not support additional local regulations regarding fireplaces and wood stoves. We follow the national guidelines on this matter.

126406.explanation: Samen Waalwijk supports a lively municipality; stricter rules do not align with that. However, attention must remain on safety and the livability of the surroundings of an event.

126407.explanation: Samen Waalwijk wants a society that is inclusive and accessible for everyone. The municipality can support businesses in making their buildings accessible.

126409.explanation: Samen Waalwijk believes good school buildings are essential for quality education. Significant investments have already been made in school buildings within the municipality. Samen Waalwijk wants to continue this policy.

126410.explanation: Samen Waalwijk sees sports clubs as indispensable to society; these clubs are places where people meet and engage in healthy activities together. Samen Waalwijk believes the municipality should support these clubs in difficult times.

126413.explanation: Samen Waalwijk views cameras as a supplement to physical enforcement, not a replacement. More camera surveillance can help, but only when it is carefully considered where it is truly necessary and what the impact is on the privacy of our residents.

126414.explanation: Samen Waalwijk believes the municipality should be there for everyone who needs care and protection. This way, they receive protection, guidance, and opportunities, giving them a chance at a safe new life.

126415.explanation: Samen Waalwijk thinks our community officers (boa's) should have appropriate weapon equipment. A baton can be part of this. Various municipalities are currently conducting trials in this area, and Samen Waalwijk wants to await their outcomes.

126420.explanation: Samen Waalwijk values participation; obligations often backfire. Tailored support is better: helping people engage in a way that suits their situation. Trust and guidance create opportunities once thought impossible.

126421.explanation: Samen Waalwijk wants a different approach to poverty, placing the human aspect at the center. By raising the income threshold for assistance, more residents can receive timely support and problems can be prevented. This contributes to the livelihood security of our residents.

126423.explanation: Samen Waalwijk sees electric cars as a development that is here to stay. The municipality must prepare public spaces accordingly by providing sufficient charging points. It is important that these facilities fit the needs of the neighborhood and environment.

126424.explanation: Samen Waalwijk chooses customized approaches and good participation with residents: determining the needs per town and neighborhood for livability, accessibility, and traffic safety. A 30 km/h zone only works if the road is designed accordingly.

126427.explanation: Samen Waalwijk stands for the freedom of choice of every resident. Everyone must be able to receive care in a way that suits them.

126429.explanation: Samen Waalwijk advocates for dignified poverty policies. Offering free menstrual products can help promote equality and health.

126431.explanation: Samen Waalwijk believes that the dog tax should be used for dog-related facilities, such as maintaining dog parks and cleaning up dog waste. It should not be a revenue source to cover other deficits. Abolishing it would lead to other tax increases.

126433.explanation: Samen Waalwijk believes Waalwijk has reached its limit with large industrial estates. No further paving over soil, but investment in quality, livability, and protection of our polders and public spaces.

126435.explanation: Samen Waalwijk actually believes our own residents should have priority for social rental homes. Therefore, Samen Waalwijk advocates a balanced distribution of social housing among all housing seekers.

126437.explanation: Samen Waalwijk values our agricultural sector and wants to preserve good farmland for food production. Thus, we do not support large solar fields on these lands. Exceptions are possible where solar panels fit well into the landscape, also within the framework of the energy transition.

126438.explanation: Samen Waalwijk opposes relocating the library. The current location is central, accessible to everyone, and owned by the municipality. Samen Waalwijk supports renovation at the current site if necessary.

126440.explanation: Samen Waalwijk places great value on the unique identity of our towns. Dialect education can contribute to culture and connection with our local history. However, this should not come at the expense of regular education and basic skills.

126441.explanation: Samen Waalwijk believes that coffee shops can have a place in our municipality, provided safety, supervision, and livability are well secured. Controlled sales are preferable to illegal street trade. Banning usually leads to more problems.

126442.explanation: Samen Waalwijk wants more control over the housing market and clear agreements about social rental housing. More social rent helps limit costs and reduce waiting lists. At the same time, we consider what residents need, as middle incomes often fall between the cracks.

126444.explanation: Samen Waalwijk wants to protect the livability in neighborhoods. These rental constructions often lead to parking pressure and nuisance.

126445.explanation: Samen Waalwijk believes tariffs should mainly cover costs and should not fill the municipal treasury. Cheaper parking also contributes to a busier city center for our entrepreneurs.

126446.explanation: Samen Waalwijk wants additional parking spaces where shortages truly exist. Additionally, smarter organization, such as clear signage and tailored approaches per neighborhood, helps make better use of available space.

126448.explanation: Samen Waalwijk thinks growth is possible, but only if it fits the scale of our towns and does not jeopardize livability. No growth for growth’s sake: first secure quality, amenities, and space, then determine how many residents Waalwijk can accommodate.

126449.explanation: Samen Waalwijk has long been very critical of the Schoenenkwartier. The municipal council has given the Schoenenkwartier a clear mandate to get things in order. We await the results and expect this task to be carried out seriously.

126450.explanation: Samen Waalwijk believes investments have mainly been made in the center of Waalwijk over recent years, with far too little attention to the towns. As far as Samen Waalwijk is concerned, the towns should receive the attention they deserve over the next 4 years!

126395.explanation: As a nuance, we note that the status of the referendum's outcome is important. We have no objection to a poll, but decision-making lies with the municipal council.

126398.explanation: Sunday begins after Saturday at 11:59 pm, therefore at 12:00 am on Sunday. A closing time on Saturday night at 11:30 pm allows most people to clean up or go home on time. This is our preference.

126403.explanation: This has never been a problem; it contributes to heating the home. Where it would cause issues between neighbors, we suggest seeking a joint solution, e.g., avoiding burning on windless days.

126406.explanation: Agreed, entire residential neighborhoods should not be forced to "enjoy" the noise from a festival or music event.

126407.explanation: This is a task for the entrepreneur themselves.

126409.explanation: The statement is too simplistic. Every council term the educational housing program is established; choices about maintenance, new construction, and renovation are made and justified within this program.

126410.explanation: Sports clubs have their own responsibility regarding fundraising, cost control, membership fees, etc. Only in exceptional cases and when a sports club has demonstrable social significance for the community may they appeal to the municipality.

126413.explanation: The question is what will happen with the camera footage. We prefer to achieve more safety by having more uniformed officers on the street.

126414.explanation: There must be adequate support for victims.

126415.explanation: They must be able to defend themselves within an increasingly violent society. The visible presence of the baton can also serve as a deterrent.

126420.explanation: Agree, as long as this does not hinder a return to paid work. Job applications, training, etc. also require time and effort, and there must remain room for that.

126421.explanation: We see increasing poverty more than a decrease here. The cost of living is rising, so we should definitely not raise the access threshold.

126423.explanation: The number of electric cars will also increase, after all.

126424.explanation: In residential neighborhoods and busy areas, the maximum speed may be 30 km/h, but not on access roads and spacious through roads.

126427.explanation: It is very important that caregiver and care recipient (m/f) understand and trust each other. The connection is more likely when they share a similar background in life. This is especially important for questions and issues around the beginning and end of our earthly life.

126429.explanation: There may and must be financial support for households and individuals who need it. Possibly, the Waalwijk Kansrijk project offers opportunities to meet this specific need.

126431.explanation: Funds are generated from this tax for the maintenance of dog walking areas. Please use these funds to tackle the problem of dog waste.

126433.explanation: The SGP is not opposed in principle to expanding business parks, but it is a question where such expansion is still possible and what type of businesses would be located here. A good topic for further research.

126435.explanation: No preferential position compared to those who have often been registered for years.

126437.explanation: Allows the owner of agricultural land to make his/her own choice in this matter.

126438.explanation: The current location of the Library is perfectly adequate.

126440.explanation: It's always good to understand the local dialect and, if possible, to speak it. When you live somewhere longer and become integrated locally, you often adopt an accent that reveals your living environment. Nothing wrong with that. Let dialects and knowledge of them not be lost.

126441.explanation: The SGP is, as is known, not a supporter of the sale and use of soft drugs.

126442.explanation: Agree, when they demonstrably contribute to reducing the housing shortage.

126444.explanation: Agree, no place for slumlords in Waalwijk. We have seen problems with housing labor migrants, think of Loefstraat, Het Fort, etc.

126445.explanation: Currently not expensive, leave as it is.

126446.explanation: Neighborhoods are often designed based on assumed or expected car ownership per household, as was common in the past. Reality is different. Provide sufficient parking facilities.

126448.explanation: When this occurs in a natural way, fine. Waalwijk MAY grow to 55,000 inhabitants, MUST is still too far for us.

126449.explanation: Seek feasibility in cost reduction and increasing visitor numbers. Subsidy is too high now.

126450.explanation: Villages may be supported financially in proportionate equality.

126395.explanation: The municipal council is elected by the residents. Moreover, in Waalwijk, participation and citizen initiative are part of policymaking wherever possible. Therefore, a referendum is not necessary.

126398.explanation: The municipality sets opening hours due to, among other things, public order and safety. During certain events and circumstances, regular opening hours must be adjustable.

126403.explanation: CDA Waalwijk wants as much uniform policy as possible regarding environmental measures (so coordination with provincial and national policy) because of support and effectiveness of local policy. This also applies to the allowance or prohibition of fireplaces and wood stoves.

126406.explanation: Where residents experience noise nuisance, permits must be stricter.

126407.explanation: By signing a UN convention, the Netherlands has committed to equal treatment of people with disabilities. CDA Waalwijk believes the government is therefore obliged to make buildings accessible, also through entrepreneurs, thus also with financial support!

126409.explanation: CDA Waalwijk wants the municipality to improve, renovate or renew school buildings where necessary. However, the municipality has invested a lot in new school buildings in recent years. New construction of some schools is currently being prepared. Improvement is therefore hardly topical.

126410.explanation: Many subsidies for sports and welfare are already granted in Waalwijk. Where there are still gaps in financial support, there is room for subsidies, provided the association is viable and cannot deploy its own resources.

126413.explanation: CDA Waalwijk wants many more police officers and public order officers on the streets. Cameras can be an important support and must certainly be deployed where they add value.

126414.explanation: CDA Waalwijk wants more attention for femicide in safety policy and protection of all residents who feel threatened. In addition, officials, public servants, teachers and caregivers must feel safe performing their duties.

126415.explanation: CDA Waalwijk wants many more police officers and public order officers on the streets. To perform their work better and defend themselves better, the baton, as far as CDA is concerned, is necessary.

126420.explanation: Regarding welfare recipients, CDA Waalwijk believes that, where possible, a contribution to society as a counterperformance may be expected.

126421.explanation: The number of households facing poverty is rising. The division between rich and poor is growing. Where the municipality can, it must offer more (financial) support to households living around the poverty line. CDA Waalwijk is therefore in favor of raising the income threshold.

126423.explanation: To further support the energy transition, the municipality must expand charging points for electric cars. The current parking policy must therefore be overhauled.

126424.explanation: Only where appropriate and coordinated with the neighborhood do we want to introduce a 30 km/h speed limit for traffic safety.

126427.explanation: If sufficient caregivers with different profiles are available, CDA Waalwijk supports the right to choose caregivers that fit one’s own religious or philosophical beliefs. However, currently there are many vacancies in care, so the choice is often not available.

126429.explanation: For residents living at or below the poverty line, this would, from CDA Waalwijk’s perspective, be a welcome financial support for their living expenses.

126431.explanation: CDA Waalwijk strives for a fair economy, solid public finances, limited burdens and a fair tax system. Limited local levies and taxes are needed for good municipal services.

126433.explanation: The current area of available business land in the municipality of Waalwijk is almost fully utilized. It is therefore good to investigate whether new business land is needed and if so, where.

126435.explanation: Status holders must register on the housing seekers list for social rental homes. Through other types of housing, such as flexible housing or similar solutions, the municipality might offer faster solutions.

126437.explanation: CDA Waalwijk opposes placing solar panels on fertile agricultural land. This results in loss of scarce productive farmland. An absolute ban is not a solution. Sometimes farmland is not profitable due to its location, making solar panel placement a good alternative.

126438.explanation: The Waalwijk library is currently housed in an excellent building. Relocation or renewal is therefore unnecessary and would lead to significant financial expenses for the Waalwijk budget, which could be much better spent on, for example, healthcare and poverty policies.

126440.explanation: Primary schools already have an overloaded schedule mandated by the national government with many priorities. Dialect is not a priority. However, arithmetic and language are, as it has been established that the level among children is steadily declining.

126441.explanation: CDA Waalwijk opposes the establishment of new coffeeshops and supports a ban.

126442.explanation: CDA Waalwijk aims for a larger share of social rental housing than the national policy, with new targets for social rental, middle-rent, and private sector housing in the municipality of Waalwijk of 35% - 40% - 25%.

126444.explanation: CDA Waalwijk is not against room rental. On the contrary, with the current housing shortage, room rental is important. CDA Waalwijk supports the strict rules for room rental, which were established by the municipality of Waalwijk on CDA Waalwijk's proposal due to nuisance complaints.

126445.explanation: In Waalwijk center, we want to reduce the number of visitor cars through paid parking. However, residents, people with physical disabilities, and entrepreneurs must still be able to park within reasonable distance from their home or work at reasonable costs.

126446.explanation: People often have more than one car. Therefore, more parking spaces must be created in the neighborhoods and on the outskirts of the center. People should be encouraged to use public transport or bicycles whenever possible.

126448.explanation: For the maintenance of local facilities and a better position of Waalwijk in the region, a limited growth in the number of residents is important.

126449.explanation: The Schoenenkwartier must first demonstrate by 2026 that the museum can attract the minimum number of 30,000 visitors. If this does not succeed, the question arises whether the current subsidy amount should be adjusted.

126450.explanation: The Waalwijk center has benefited in recent years from a multi-million budget to give the center a new look. This means it is realistic to now allocate a comparable budget to Waspik and Sprang-Capelle to invest in these communities or their centers.

126395.explanation: D66 considers citizen participation very important. Plans improve from it. Democratic renewal is high on the agenda. The referendum is only one of the tools and often leads to polarization. D66 prefers cooperation.

126398.explanation: As a liberal party, D66 supports space for entrepreneurs. In the case of cafés and restaurants, we also consider the interests of local residents. On weekends, closing times may be extended to 03:00 hours, also to meet the wishes of our youth.

126403.explanation: Waalwijk has joined the Clean Air Agreement. Clean air is important to D66. Besides wood burning being cozy, it also causes much air pollution that can harm health. D66 therefore wants to discourage wood burning-especially during calm weather.

126406.explanation: There are already strict noise standards for festivals and parties. Here, too, a good balance must be found between livability in the area and the interests of the event or festival.

126407.explanation: An entrepreneur is responsible for the accessibility of their premises. D66 wants to contribute to the realization of wheelchair-accessible disabled toilets, provided these facilities are publicly accessible to everyone.

126409.explanation: Almost all schools in the municipality of Waalwijk have now been replaced or renovated. A few schools will be addressed in the coming years. D66 is pleased with this!

126410.explanation: D66 holds sports clubs close to its heart. They are important for health and community cohesion. The municipality supports sports clubs with various subsidies, for example for youth or contributions to facilities. In exceptional cases, the municipality can do more.

126413.explanation: Cameras create an illusion of safety. D66 wants to install cameras only if this is temporary, targeted, and proportional.

126414.explanation: D66 considers safety for women and children very important. Waalwijk has already arranged this regionally.

126415.explanation: D66 values neighborhood enforcement officers (wijk-BOA’s) highly. They serve as the eyes and ears in our neighborhoods. They reduce the gap between citizens and authorities and increase the feeling of safety. Backup from the police must be adequate.

126420.explanation: This is already happening in Waalwijk. D66 also finds this important. Because those who work feel better. Work provides livelihood security, as well as social contacts, purpose, and personal development. The goal of the reciprocal obligation is to prepare for paid work.

126421.explanation: D66 believes that work should pay off. It is also good to care for our residents with minimum income. The income limit to receive assistance for poverty should therefore be raised to 130%.

126423.explanation: Electric driving is the future. To ensure more people switch to electric vehicles, D66 focuses on increasing public charging points, for example in the centers of Sprang-Capelle, Waspik, and Waalwijk (think of De Els, Taxandriaweg, schools, and the retail park).

126424.explanation: D66 wants 30 km/h to be the standard speed in the residential neighborhoods in our municipality. On the main thoroughfares, keeping 50 km/h is appropriate. To promote cycling, D66 wants to create more bicycle streets where cars are guests and cyclists have priority.

126427.explanation: D66 is a liberal party and emphasizes freedom of choice. Regarding healthcare, the municipality does not have authority. Care entrepreneurs take the lead, and residents can choose. For WMO assistance or youth care, quality and professionalism are always top priorities for D66.

126429.explanation: D66 recognizes that tampons and sanitary pads are not accessible to every woman or girl. This can lead to school dropout and/or social exclusion. D66 would like to give extra attention to this issue.

126431.explanation: D66 strives for a clear dog policy. This means challenging dog owners to minimize nuisance. The municipality provides good facilities in this regard. As far as D66 is concerned, the dog tax is an outdated levy.

126433.explanation: For D66, the economy is important but not at all costs. Waalwijk already has many industrial areas. By thinking creatively about better utilizing the available space (more compact and upwards), much more can be done on the same land. Current developments will be completed.

126435.explanation: The law states that every municipality must offer housing to a certain number of status holders annually. D66 agrees that we should meet our legal (humanitarian) obligations. It also helps integrate our new residents properly into our society.

126437.explanation: D66 supports the generation of sustainable energy. Solar panels on sustainable agricultural land are not the most logical solution. Roofs of homes, businesses, parking lots, and leftover land are more appropriate. D66 is open to innovative solutions.

126438.explanation: The library is at the heart of the community. The location of the library is important. The current location is good but can be much better. Therefore, D66 supports relocating and modernizing the library closer to the center. Of course, accessibility for all groups must be taken into account.

126440.explanation: D66 is a liberal party. It is up to the schools and parents whether or not to pay attention to this.

126441.explanation: Closing coffeeshops leads to more street dealing and nuisance. D66 finds it important that residents of the municipality of Waalwijk have the freedom to use soft drugs safely.

126442.explanation: Waalwijk currently has more than 30% social rental housing. That is more than most other municipalities. D66 thinks it is important to maintain this and therefore puts great effort into housing construction.

126444.explanation: D66 believes that homes in our municipality should be intended for households and preferably not as investment objects or room rental. Waalwijk has strict rules for room rental. D66 wants to keep it that way for now.

126445.explanation: Paid parking serves a purpose: to ensure the city remains accessible, encourage more people to walk or cycle to the center, and prevent parking nuisance for shops and residents in the center.

126446.explanation: For D66, it is important to keep space in our living environment for movement, play, nature, water, and housing. That means making good choices and not always prioritizing cars. We already have many parking spaces.

126448.explanation: D66 supports growth for Waalwijk. This is important for maintaining our good facilities. But growth is not a goal in itself for D66.

126449.explanation: D66 believes the Schoenenkwartier is a calling card for our municipality. It makes our history tangible and connects with today and tomorrow.

126450.explanation: D66 finds it important to invest in all our communities. Waspik will get a solution for the sports clubs and the sports hall. In Sprang-Capelle, Zidewinde needs necessary renewal. And Waalwijk also faces challenges requiring investment.

126395.explanation: Referendums are not a solution for complex political issues. In the case of a consultative referendum, the outcome is not binding; this causes confusion and dissatisfaction. Participation, a survey, and/or a panel are much better ways to gauge residents' opinions and involve them in decision-making.

126398.explanation: Cafés and restaurants are always located in areas where people live and therefore also sleep. It is quite common for departing patrons to cause (unintentional) disturbances.

126403.explanation: The air quality in the municipality does not meet international health standards (WHO). This is mainly due to traffic on the A59 plus the N261 and industry. A fireplace and wood stove provide cozy warmth but worsen air quality, especially when it is windless and/or foggy.

126406.explanation: Events are generally good for social cohesion and provide entertainment that would otherwise be missing. However, noise nuisance should certainly not continue until the early hours.

126407.explanation: It is part of the (social) responsibility of entrepreneurs to make their buildings accessible. The Christian Union is fine with the municipality helping them with knowledge and advice, but not by providing financial support.

126409.explanation: The municipality of Waalwijk has education housing well organized. Attention is already given to sustainability and a good indoor climate. When buildings get too old, the municipality makes funds available for replacement construction according to agreements.

126410.explanation: The municipality of Waalwijk already contributes financially a lot to sports by not charging for many costs related to indoor and outdoor facilities. The Christian Union wants sport to be accessible to everyone, especially youth, without financial and physical barriers.

126413.explanation: There is already a lot of camera surveillance in Waalwijk, for example in the center and in industrial areas. More is really not necessary.

126414.explanation: It is a task to protect residents as well as possible. Therefore, active efforts must be made to combat femicide, (sexual) transgressive behavior, stalking, and domestic violence. A safe place must be available, but it does not have to be a women's shelter.

126415.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) must be able to do their work safely. Therefore, they wear bodycams. The use of weapons is reserved for the police, who are available to BOAs on call in serious situations.

126420.explanation: This sounds too much like having to provide a counterperformance. Not every benefit recipient can do that. However, social activation should be encouraged so that benefit recipients go outside, meet other people, do volunteer work, and possibly move towards paid employment.

126421.explanation: The government must be there for people living in poverty. It is better to provide targeted support for this, for example (financially) assisting in making homes more sustainable to combat energy poverty. Generic measures like raising an income limit are too expensive.

126423.explanation: The municipality of Waalwijk is already busy realizing charging stations, as the number of electric cars will continue to increase. Charging points for electric bikes should not be forgotten either.

126424.explanation: A maximum speed of 30 km/h within the built-up area, in the Christian Union's view, increases livability and safety. Only on through roads with separate bicycle paths should a maximum of 50 km/h apply.

126427.explanation: Always is very absolute. But it is nice if people who need care get caregivers who match their faith, culture, or beliefs. Because needing care brings questions of life and meaning.

126429.explanation: This is not really a municipal task. Poverty care is.

126431.explanation: The municipality of Waalwijk incurs necessary costs for dog owners, such as dog toilets and exercise areas, a machine for cleaning up dog poop, plus BOAs responsible for enforcement. These costs can be covered by the proceeds of the dog tax.

126433.explanation: The municipality of Waalwijk has hardly any unemployment but does have great housing shortage. With the attraction of companies, the pressure on the housing market only increases. Only for moving companies from residential areas to places like Haven 8 can an exception be made.

126435.explanation: The asylum seekers' centers must be emptied. Refugees with a residence permit still have no roof over their heads and, as far as the Christian Union is concerned, should be given priority in getting a (permanent) home.

126437.explanation: Good agricultural land is scarce. Besides roofs, solar panels can be installed in other places, such as alongside roads.

126438.explanation: If by renewing is meant relocating, the Christian Union disagrees. The existing library on Wilhelminastraat may get a renovation so that this meeting place better fits with the current times.

126440.explanation: This is not a task for schools. Education already has to do a lot, partly to the detriment of language and arithmetic education.

126441.explanation: Alcohol, drugs, and gambling ruin many lives. The Christian Union wants to combat addiction and drug problems, including hemp plantations, drug labs, and coffee shops. Because cannabis nowadays is no longer a soft drug but is addictive.

126442.explanation: The municipality of Waalwijk has agreed to adhere to the national standard of 30% social rental housing. Given the demand, it is important to build sufficient homes for people with lower incomes.

126444.explanation: Renting out individual rooms or multiple people sharing houses can be a solution to the housing problem. But exploiting people by charging far too much rent for a room must be prohibited.

126445.explanation: Parking rates must be cost-covering. At the moment, the municipality earns money from parking. Therefore, there is reason to make it somewhat cheaper.

126446.explanation: The problem is more that there are too many cars, which are also used for short rides. More parking spaces come at the expense of greenery. Only in exceptional cases (old streets) may it be necessary to create more parking places.

126448.explanation: Growth in the number of inhabitants does not have to be a goal. Building more homes to meet current needs, such as for starters and seniors, is.

126449.explanation: The Schoenenkwartier belongs to Waalwijk. A large part of the subsidy is needed for housing and a small group of staff. The museum cannot operate with a lower subsidy.

126450.explanation: Already tens of millions have been invested in various ways in the center of Waalwijk. The plans for the coming years cost far too much, while in Sprang-Capelle and Waspik, for example, new sports halls are badly needed.

statements.title: More money should go to the villages and less to Waalwijk's centre.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should enable all primary schools to give attention to the dialect of Waalwijk, Sprang-Capelle and Waspik.
The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Waalwijk.

titleParts.text: In Waalwijk moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Waalwijk.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality of Waalwijk should grow from 50,000 to 55,000 inhabitants.
The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who monitor safety in Waalwijk should be issued a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Waalwijk controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The business parks in Waalwijk should be allowed to expand.
A shelter for victims of domestic violence should be established in Waalwijk.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The municipality should ban the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should renovate the library in Waalwijk.
At least 35% of newly built homes in Waalwijk should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands currently 28% of homes are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Waalwijk moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Paid parking in the centre of Waalwijk should be made cheaper.
The residents of Waalwijk should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Waalwijk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The subsidy for the Schoenenkwartier in Waalwijk should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: voor het Schoenenkwartier in Waalwijk moet omlaag.
